mnemonics.title: DEPLOY
mnemonics.content:
D - Document everything
E - Evaluate with canary
P - Plan rollback strategy
L - Log and monitor
O - Orchestrate with containers
Y - Yield to traffic gradually

mnemonics.title: BLUE-GREEN
mnemonics.content:
B - Backup environment ready
L - Load balancer switch
U - Update green environment
E - Execute health checks
G - Go live by switching
R - Revert if issues
E - Eliminate old after success
E - Ensure zero-downtime
N - Notify stakeholders

acronyms.usage: Automated pipeline for building, testing, and deploying code changes
Managing infrastructure through configuration files for automation
Distributed network for delivering content with low latency
Distributing traffic across multiple servers for high availability
Maximum acceptable data loss measured in time
Maximum acceptable time to restore service after disruption

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two identical stadiums (blue and green). During a game, switch from one to another instantly - fans (traffic) don't notice the change! One is always active while the other gets renovated.
Think of a canary in a coal mine - send a small 'bird' (new version) first to test safety. If it survives, send more. If it dies, retreat immediately!
Picture replacing wheels on a moving car one at a time - the car (application) stays moving (available) while wheels (instances) are updated sequentially.
A factory assembly line: code enters one end, gets built, tested, and packaged automatically, then deploys as a finished product at the other end.

analogies.analogy: Like having a spare tire - when one goes flat (fails), you instantly switch to the spare without stopping your journey.
Like taste-testing a new recipe for a large dinner party - you try a small portion first before serving everyone.
Like a symphony orchestra conductor - coordinates many musicians (containers) to play in harmony without missing a beat.
Like a recipe - the same ingredients (code) always produce the same dish (infrastructure), regardless of who cooks.
Like light switches in a house - you can turn features on/off instantly without rebuilding the house.
Like security checkpoints at airports - only allow passengers (traffic) through if they pass the health verification.
